title: SURGE: Approximation-free Training Free Particle Filter for Diffusion Surrogate
core_claim: The paper proposes a gradient-free, inference-time resampling method for diffusion models using Girsanov-based path-wise importance weights, claiming it avoids repeated score, gradient, Hessian, or PDE evaluations while matching particle-level SMC behavior and improving sample quality.
triage_rationale: This looks like a technically substantive methods paper in a current ML area, with a clear algorithmic contribution and a plausible experimental payoff. It is worth review because the abstract makes a strong claim about an unbiased equivalence and simpler inference-time guidance, which is both research-relevant and potentially useful to critique on podcast.
likely_red_flags: Strong theoretical equivalence claim in the abstract without visible qualification
Performance claims are broad and benchmark-based but unsupported here
Title/abstract suggest a renamed method and may hide incremental overlap with prior guidance/resampling work
